text: Code reviews are a vital practice in software development where developers examine each other's code before it is merged. The main advantage is catching bugs early in the development process, which saves both time and money. Instead of discovering errors in later stages or in production, team members can identify and fix them immediately. Additionally, code reviews promote knowledge sharing among teammates. Junior developers learn best practices from experienced colleagues, while everyone stays informed about different parts of the project. Ultimately, this collaborative process improves overall team quality by encouraging higher standards, better communication, and a shared sense of responsibility for the codebase.
